GOOG.reasoning: VOTE COUNT: 1 bullish (swing_breakout, 62), 1 bearish (swing_mean_reversion, 52), 3 neutral (swing_trend_momentum 30, swing_catalyst_news 25, swing_macro_context 62). No directional majority. The most striking feature of this panel is that the 3 neutral agents are not confused — they are all saying the same thing: the underlying thesis is valid but the entry is wrong right now. swing_trend_momentum is emphatically bullish on the trend (ADX=63.92, EMA fan textbook) but will not chase — it is waiting for a pullback to EMA10 ~$347-350. swing_macro_context finds the macro regime supportive but the R/R math fails 2:1 at current prices (0.79:1 to nearest support). swing_catalyst_news notes the post-earnings catalyst is already fully priced (the +10% gap-up on Apr 30 is the reaction event, not the setup). Together these three neutral votes form a coherent message: GOOG is the best stock in the basket, it just ran 10% in one day and is not a buyable setup today. The breakout agent is technically correct that a daily close above resistance on 2.6x volume is a valid breakout — but it also acknowledges the measured move target ($364) is already exceeded and RSI is at 97.75, which makes chasing after the fact a low-conviction trade. The mean reversion agent fires the fade structure correctly (Z-score 3.29, pct_b 1.14, both timeframes overbought) but per its own rules requires a reversal candle before entry — none exists. LESSONS APPLIED: The NVDA loss (Apr 30, -$264.65, EMA pullback Fib dip-buy) is a direct pattern-match: high RSI (81-87 on NVDA vs 86-98 on GOOG), no confirmation candle, stretched macro environment. This is the only setup-type in the lessons table and it has a 0% win rate. GOOG's RSI is more extreme than NVDA's at the time of that loss. This dials confidence down on any entry attempt now. WIKI CONTEXT: No open GOOG position (both prior trades abandoned Apr 29). Prior trade #14 was entered at $331.73 on a clean breakout above $330.58 — that structure was similar to today's but the entry was made at the breakout level, not 15% above it. The prior trade hit its $347.50 target. The lesson from prior GOOG history is: enter near the breakout level, not after a gap extension. CONCLUSION: Wait for a pullback. The 3 neutral agents converge on the $342-358 zone as the re-entry window (swing_macro_context: 342-358 for 3:1+ R/R; swing_trend_momentum: EMA10 ~$347-350; swing_breakout: breakout re-test ~$353-358). Any entry in that zone would convert this to a high-conviction long. Today, at $381.94, the trade does not meet the minimum criteria of any strategy agent except swing_breakout — and even that agent acknowledges the original setup has been superseded. Hold cash, set alerts at $353-358 for re-evaluation.
GOOG.key_conflicts: The core conflict is swing_breakout (bullish, 62) vs. swing_mean_reversion (bearish, 52) — one wants to buy the confirmed breakout, the other wants to fade the extreme overbought extension. This conflict is highly informative: it means the trend has transitioned from 'breakout just occurred' to 'breakout is now extended.' swing_breakout is right that the April 30 volume-confirmed daily close is a legitimate breakout event; swing_mean_reversion is right that RSI 97.75 and Z-score 3.29 with no reversal candle are not entry conditions for a new fade either. The resolution: neither side has a clean entry today. swing_trend_momentum and swing_macro_context reinforce this — both are structurally bullish on GOOG but refuse to chase 10% above the EMA. The three neutral agents are not split on direction; they are split on timing. A second conflict worth noting: swing_breakout uses $381.94 as its entry (current market), while swing_macro_context and swing_trend_momentum use $349-355 (pullback zone). This level divergence of ~8% is a red flag — when agents are this far apart on entry, it signals the current price is 'no man's land.' Finally, lessons dial down the sole bullish vote: the NVDA EMA pullback Fib dip-buy setup failed at RSI 81-87, and GOOG's RSI (97.75) exceeds that. A 3-of-5 bullish panel on this setup-type with a 0% lessons win rate would still warrant a neutral call — the actual 1-of-5 bullish reading makes neutral the only defensible outcome.
